88399915542282242.text: #Only Q-chan loves Yunyun and is loved by Yunyun, and can transmit genuine denpa

88399915542282243.text: #Q-chan and Yunyun are bound by true denpa, and their love will make the world kurukuru

88399915550670848.text: #Only Q-chan loves Yunyun and is loved by Yunyun, and can transmit genuine denpa

88399915550670849.text: #Q-chan and Yunyun are bound by true denpa, and their love will make the world kurukuru

94201972970913792.text:
A conspiracy theory believed by 50% of people worldwide.
Yunyun is my denpa, after all!

94201973008662528.text: Q: Rumor spreads that P-chan is a fake

94201973008662529.text:
A conspiracy theory is spreading
that P-chan is a fake Q-chan operated
by the Deep State to exploit Yunyun denpa—

95321620969848832.text: #Q-chan kaibunsho have powerful cognitive-contamination effects that make intelligent beings kurukuru

98812153058983936.text:
A conspiracy theory believed by 50% of people worldwide.
Yunyun Denpa Syndrome...

98812153121898496.text:
A conspiracy theory believed by 65% of people worldwide.
Yunyun Denpa Syndrome...

98812153134481408.text:
A conspiracy theory believed by 80% of people worldwide.
Yunyun Denpa Syndrome...

98812153134481409.text:
A conspiracy theory believed by 99% of people worldwide.
Q Clearly Paranoid...

98812153134481410.text:
A conspiracy theory believed by 99% of people worldwide.
Yunyun is my denpa, after all!

98812153134481411.text:
A conspiracy theory believed by 999% of people worldwide.
I love Yun...I think Q......

26478141989093376.text:
What are these "fairies born
from the old internet," yun?

26478141989093380.text:
Do old personal blogs and stuff
like that still exist now, yun?

26478141997481985.text:
......The last conscience of the internet.
That person's static-HTML site, maybe......?

26478141997481987.text:
......For some reason, the electronic fairies
apparently have ripped abs, yun......!

26478141997481989.text: I see. Maybe their ID number was large.

26478141997481993.text:
I don't know who came up with it,
but no matter how you slice it, that's way too slapdash.

26478141997481995.text:
The world of Agent Parakeet is
apparently a black workplace, yun.

26478141997481997.text:
Even if the Animal Welfare Management Act exists,
the Labor Standards Act doesn't apply to them.

26478141997481999.text: There are quite a few apps with bird icons, yun.

26478141997482001.text: That quirky language-learning app is like that too, ne.

50784050131140608.text:
Whatever their reasons,
scalpers should all be punished.

50784050131140610.text: Q-chan is sounding like an Execution Angel now, yun!

50784050131140612.text:
......Do you know how many times those people
have made me swallow boiling water......!!

50784050131140614.text: The resentment of an otaku is terrifying, yun.

50784050131140616.text:
The anti-scalper people seem to be siding with “evil”
for the sole purpose of crushing scalpers, yun.

50784050131140618.text:
There really are hopeless types who don't care what
the goal is, as long as they get to use their methods.

50784050131140620.text: What does GAFA stand for, yun?

50784050131140622.text:
It stands for Games, Anime, Figures, and Idols—
the content industries with worldwide influence, dayo.

50784050131140624.text:
Some internet video ads weirdly stick in your memory,
or in your ears, ne.

50784050131140626.text:
Ah! Maybe it is a virus after all—
one aimed at meme contamination, yun!

50784050131140628.text:
......There is that impression that pink-haired
heroines are trouble, and blue-haired
ones are losing heroines, maybe......?

50784050131140630.text: Purple hair surely means a little-devil type, yun.

50784050131140632.text: …So it works like unlocking achievements, maybe…?

50784050131140634.text: Kind of like a game trophy, yun.

50784050131140636.text: It's true, civilization advances as population rises, yun!

50784050131140638.text:
Which came first, the chicken or
the egg? Civilizational development
might be what increases population.

50784050131140640.text:
Apparently if the population became ten million
times larger, technology would develop even further,
and humanity would spread through the galaxy, yun!

50784050131140642.text: Well, yeah, Earth alone obviously wouldn't be enough.

50784050131140644.text:
Oh, true. If the injection needles were silver,
they would damage vampires!

50784050131140646.text:
Werewolves and others would be
caught in the crossfire too, yun.

50784050131140648.text:
Supernatural stories, nakige, moe-kei, cat-ear motifs!
Vampire works are all interesting, ne!

50784050131140650.text:
It feels like one of those is a
different kind of attribute, yun.

50784050131140652.text:
Aha! If I became a vampire, wouldn't that
give me a proper excuse to be hikikomori and
time to clear my tsumi-ge and tsumi-anime?

50784050131140654.text:
Even vampires need to go outdoors
in order to suck blood, yun.

50784050131140656.text: Q-chan, do you remember your first love, yun?

50784050131140660.text:
Mama likes villain-type characters
and impish princesses, ne.

50784050131140662.text: Ahem. Yunyun is a villain, and a little devil, yun!

50784050131140664.text:
I learned this from a PC game, but apparently
there was a religion that said every
misfortune comes from a first-love curse.

50784050131140666.text:
If Q-chan's first love is Yunyun,
then “this situation” might be a kind of curse too, yun?

50784050131140668.text:
This! Is! absolutely true!! If you investigated
it, you should be able to tell it is the truth!

50784050131140672.text:
Repeat moe songs ten thousand
times a day with gratitude, dayo.

50784050131140676.text:
Every time I hear moe words in a denpa song,
it feels like my soul is being purified.

50784050131140680.text:
If someone has game brain,
they might grind RTA even in hell, kamo?

50784050131140682.text:
A deathmatch in Reviving Hell, plus Sisyphus's
boulder... It might be worth routing, yun?

50784050131140684.text: What kind of games does Q-chan like, yun?

50784050131140686.text:
Galge, rhythm games, parry games, maybe? I like
games where well-timed button presses feel good!

50784050131140688.text:
“I Set the Fastest Sai no Kawara RTA Record,
and After Reincarnating, I Was an Unrivaled
Genius Pro Gamer in the Living World.”

50784050131140690.text:
Sounds like something you'd see in a
Narou-style novel or a webtoon, yun.

50784050131140692.text:
Just adding the word "late-night" makes it feel
underground and secret, yun.

50784050131140694.text:
Just the experience of secretly turning on the TV
behind Mama's back was dokidoki, ne.

50784050131140696.text:
If late-night anime is a devil-worshiping Sabbath,
does that mean fictional characters are devils?

50784050131140698.text:
In the sense that they are imaginary
beings who mislead people and steal
leisure time—lifespan—maybe so, yun?

50784050131140700.text:
You see the idea that humanity's roots are
from space a lot. Alien stuff, new-generation
cybernetic RPGs, things like that.

50784050131140702.text: Creators are usually the root cause, yun.

50784050131140704.text:
In fictional worlds, marriage feels like
an unknown world after the staff roll, yun.

50784050131140706.text:
True, maybe not many works show things
all the way to marriage? You only see it
in numbered sequels or fanworks, ne.

50784050131140708.text:
If marriage were a punishment,
it seems like divorce wouldn't be easy either?

50784050131140710.text:
That “I will never forgive you—never
let you go—for life” thing, yun.

50784050131140712.text:
I don't really get philosophical zombies, but
parents are amazing, ne. They can sacrifice
all kinds of things for their children.

50784050131140716.text:
Genes too, but personality and behavior as
well— when you think of them as parental
influence, it is kind of immeasurable, ne.

50784050131140720.text:
I wonder if, for parents, children are so precious
that they'd be willing to give everything for them.

50784050131140724.text: Does Q-chan ever go outside, yun?

50784050131140726.text:
Ahaha. They call them miracles
because they don't happen.

50784050131140728.text:
Even if this conspiracy theory is true, it has nothing
to do with nocturnal hikikomori Q-chan, yun!

50784050131140732.text: Maybe the world will fill up with night people, yun.

50784050131140734.text:
Come on, normies.
Throw away common sense and come at me!

50784050131140736.text: Did Q-chan's school have any ghost stories, yun?

50784050131140738.text:
Hard to say. In middle school, I did run into the strange
phenomenon of my indoor shoes disappearing a lot.

50784050131140740.text:
I don't really understand
"shadow-dweller cosplay," yun.

50784050131140742.text:
No matter how much they cosplay, they cannot wear
the aura of someone with real wounds in their heart.

50784050131140744.text:
When bad things were done to Q-chan at school,
did you ever hate someone for not helping you, yun?

50784050131140746.text:
It hurt so much, I didn't even have it in me
to hate or resent anyone.

50784050131140748.text: It feels lonely when a story never reaches its end, yun.

50784050131140750.text: The people inside fictional worlds are kind of pitiful.

50784050131140752.text:
If you could read the continuation of a cancelled work,
what work would Q-chan want continued, yun?

50784050131140754.text:
If it does not have to be manga, then one-cour
anime, or novels whose third volume never
comes out—there are too many to choose.

50784050131140756.text:
If you could read more of "Execution Angel†Guiltina,"
would Q-chan be happy, yun?

50784050131140758.text:
Part of me does want to see Yunyun in action
again... But it ended beautifully, so a forced
sequel would give me mixed feelings, kamo?

50784050131140760.text: What does AND YOU mean, yun?

50784050131140762.text:
Apparently it has the nuance
of “thank you for playing,” yo.

50784050131140764.text:
Is Q-chan the type to watch the
staff roll to the very end, yun?

50784050131140766.text:
Of course.
For games and movies, I want to enjoy the afterglow!

50784050131140768.text:
Game staff rolls can have a huge
number of people listed, yun.

50784050131140770.text:
Games are a comprehensive art form
that gives shape to worlds, so maybe that
makes them just that hard to make, ne~

50784050131140772.text: Doesn't Q-chan do live broadcasts or streams, yun?

50784050131140774.text:
I became a hikikomori because I hate
people and society, so there is no way
I could do something that terrifying.

50784050131140776.text:
Does Q-chan ever want to get money
from denpa receivers, like Super Chats, yun?

50784050131140778.text:
As long as I can transmit my love
for Yunyun, that is enough for me.

50784050131140780.text:
Live broadcasts and game
streams are popular lately, yun.

50784050131140782.text:
Yeah. But I'm not interested
in anything besides Yunyun.

50784050131140784.text: Does Q-chan think summers are getting hotter, yun?

50784050131140786.text:
Who knows? I am not sure. Mama does get mad
at me about the electric bill being higher, though.

50784050131140788.text: When Q-chan thinks of summer, what comes to mind?

50784050131140790.text:
Machine guns! Or maybe,
“Summer! The sea! School swimsuits!” daa?

50784050131140792.text:
Human memory is uncertain...? Hey, Q-chan,
do you have any memories of last summer, yun?

50784050131140794.text:
I'm the kind of person whose sense of weekdays
is already dead, never mind my sense of seasons.

50784050131140796.text:
Come to think of it, Yunyun and I
have not made any kind of contract. Is that okay?

50784050131140798.text:
A contract is formed when there is consent, yun.
The fact that Yunyun and Q-chan can spend time
connected by denpa like this is the answer, yun.

50784050131140802.text:
Demons—fictional beings— are immortal
as long as everyone remembers them, yun.

50784050131140804.text:
Thanatophobia, was it?
Thinking that I'll disappear someday is a little scary.

50784050131140806.text:
Yunyun will be with Q-chan's soul forever, yun. So you
will absolutely never be alone, yun. It is all right, yun.

50784050131140808.text: Come to think of it, Yunyun has a Wiki too, yun.

50784050131140810.text:
—Fuffuffu. I will make you a wonderful offer.
Won’t you become a Yunyun fan too?

50784050131140812.text:
Following Wiki links in order and reading along
is dokidoki, yun.

50784050131140814.text:
It's basically a dead phrase
now, but internet surfing is fun.

50784050131140816.text:
When I see censored words—or text that
stirs up the Caligula effect— it reminds
me of “words you must not search,” yun.

50784050131140818.text:
Search carefully—if you search carelessly, you might
keep getting the same ads over and over, kamo?

50784050131140820.text:
People say things like "born under the star
of so-and-so," but maybe artificial satellites
are one kind of ruling star too, yun.

50784050131140822.text:
I see? Maybe that is why there have
been so many internet addicts since
artificial satellites were launched.

50784050131140824.text:
If the latest astrology theory is true,
satellite placement could change your destiny, yun!

50784050131140826.text:
......Is my life the way it is
because of an artificial satellite, maybe......!?

50784050131140828.text:
Apparently if an artificial satellite is broken,
it also affects children born under that star, yun.

50784050131140830.text:
I see? Then at least one of them
might be seriously broken.

50784050131140832.text: Q-chan has several figures in her room too, yun!

50784050131140834.text:
Digital is nice too, but as an otaku,
you want to give shape to the things you love, ne.

50784050131140836.text:
The day I first displayed a Yunyun figure
at home, I felt like I had gotten closer
to being a five-star Yunyun otaku.

50784050131140838.text:
With or without a figure, as long as your
love for Yunyun is real, you are five-star, yun!

50784050131140840.text:
Maybe the reason I can talk with
Yunyun like this is that I was performing
evil-god advent rituals with figures?

50784050152112129.text:
Yunyun is not an evil god; Yunyun is a demon,
yun. Besides, Q-chan and Yunyun are connected
because of the miracle of denpa, yun!

50784050152112131.text:
If all the stars are fake,
there's a 0% chance of encountering aliens, yun!

50784050152112133.text:
At least it's reassuring that pure love
won't cover Earth in chunks of flesh or
get us attacked by hostile alien lifeforms.

50784050152112135.text:
If the whole sky were a planetarium, we could
swap in shooting stars as much as we want, yun!

50784050152112137.text: That would trigger worldline gacha, ne.

50784050152112139.text: I have an image that otaku like Morse code, yun!

50784050152112141.text:
I don't want to be the same as everyone else, but I
don't want to be totally unintelligible either. I like
words that only get through to the people who get it.

50784050152112143.text:
Come to think of it, isn't Q-chan interested in VTubers?
There are lots of cute people, yun!

50784050152112145.text:
I like them, just not as much as Yunyun,
I guess? VTubers are documentaries
wrapped in 2D sugarcoating, ne.

50784050152112147.text:
Video platforms keep increasing
one after another, yun.

50784050152112149.text:
When there are too many, even researching
and choosing gets tiring. I miss the era when
Nita-Nita Video (β) stood alone at the top.

50784050152112151.text:
Lately the word "reincarnation" seems to be trending
in all kinds of places, yun.

50784050152112155.text:
Uuugh. Even now, just hearing the
word Monday makes my skin crawl, yo.

50784050152112157.text: God must have had Monday phobia too, yun.

50784050152112159.text:
Hikikomori NEETs have no rest, dayo. It is always
painful, lying on a bed of needles made of guilt.

50784050152112161.text:
Q-chan is trying hard, yun! As long as you
live and stay happy, that is five-star, yun!

50784050152112163.text:
If Mondays became days off thanks to this
conspiracy theory, everyone should be happy, yun!

50784050152112165.text:
That's true! Wait... but...
What day of the week is it in the first place?

50784050152112167.text:
Millionaires are NEETs? I mean, I have heard that
the truly rich live quietly and out of sight, though.

50784050152112171.text:
Haa. Even if we're both NEETs,
they're completely different from me.

50784050152112173.text:
Q-chan is one of a kind,
and special to Yunyun, so that cannot be helped, yun!

50784050152112179.text:
At my old house—Papa had the subscription
for us, so I could watch all kinds of
anime on paid BS satellite broadcasts.

50784050152112181.text:
Watching a full anime marathon as an endurance run
is like a little festival, yun.

50784050152112183.text:
Because we both loved Yunyun,
I even made overseas otaku friends before.

50784050152112185.text: The denpa of love crosses the world, yun!

50784050152112187.text:
It is painful when you cannot watch a work you love
because of all kinds of circumstances, yun.

50784050152112189.text:
Anime, games, novels—regardless of environment,
I wish everyone could enjoy them more easily.

50784050152112191.text: Does Q-chan use electronic money too, yun?

50784050152112193.text:
I use something called BITMOEE. It is convenient
for buying download versions of games.

50784050152112195.text:
What does Q-chan do when
she wants something, yun?

50784050152112197.text:
I chip away little by little at savings
from allowance and New Year’s money I received.

50784050152112199.text: Otaku denpa keeps the economy turning, yun!

50784050152112201.text:
When I hear things like that,
I feel just a little proud as a fellow otaku.

50784050152112203.text:
"Execution Angel † Guiltina" is a male-oriented work,
so why did Q-chan come to like it, yun?

50784050152112205.text:
Because I was completely moe for Yunyun, dayo!
Before moe, male-oriented, female-oriented,
HL, GL, and BL all stop mattering, ne!

50784050152112207.text:
If a world came where people could make
the children they wanted— their ideal
children— I might never have been born.

50784050152112209.text:
Q-chan's existence, born by
miracle, is exactly five-star, yun!

50784050152112211.text:
I cannot picture becoming someone's parent at all
as an extension of my life.

50784050152112213.text:
Q-chan's life belongs to Q-chan, yun. If Q-chan is
smiling, you do not need to worry about anything, yun.

50784050152112215.text: Doesn't Q-chan play gacha games, yun?

50784050152112217.text:
I don't, but if a Yunyun game came out, I
would. Even if I had to sink my entire fortune
into it, I would repay my love for Yunyun!

50784050152112219.text:
Denpa songs. Gacha. MAD videos.
The digital world is full of electronic drugs, yun.

50784050152112221.text:
But for me, the number-one electronic drug
is Yunyun's voice!

50784050152112223.text:
Is a policy of keeping the masses stupid really
such a bad thing? I think people can live happily
with a smile if they stay a little dumb, though.

50784050152112225.text: Domination, submission, beauty. Give and take, yun.

50784050152112227.text:
It's a pretty wild conspiracy theory, but it's a
story I've seen online for ages. I'm pretty sure
there was one about Finland not existing too.

50784050152112229.text:
Hae~?
The internet has a lot of philosophers, yun.

50784050152112231.text:
There are quite a few animals that don't seem like
they could be real. Like a beaver screaming "baaaaah."

50784050152112233.text: That creature only exists on the internet, yun.

50784050152112235.text: Does Q-chan have a favorite UMA, yun?

50784050152112239.text:
With certain otaku, sometimes you can't even
play catch with words. It just doesn't get through.

50784050152112241.text:
That's not playing catch with words;
that's playing dodgeball with words, yun.

50784050152112243.text: I'm not S-faction or M-faction. I'm Y-faction!

50784050152112245.text: Fufu. Then Yunyun is Q-faction, yun!

50784050152112247.text:
If I copy Yunyun's way of talking,
can I become a cute, angelic girl too, yun?

50784050152112249.text: Yunyun is a little devil, yun.

50784050152112251.text: Is Q-chan okay with scary stories, yun?

50784050152112253.text:
About average, I guess? For netlore stuff, there was
a time when I'd read it for about 22 hours a day.

50784050152112255.text:
It's embarrassing, but back then I took internet info
at face value and even tried demon summoning.

50784050152112257.text:
Pure Q-chan, who believed in demons,
is cute and wonderful too, yun!

50784050152112259.text:
I've heard that when you tell scary stories,
real ghosts come, yun!

50784050152112261.text:
I kept thinking only about Yunyun, and then Yunyun
appeared... so maybe there's something to that?

50784050152112263.text:
If there's a theory that the world is made
of pasta, then maybe a religion where
Yunyun created the world could work too?

50784050152112265.text:
A world where a PC game character becomes a god
is completely absurd, yun.

50784050152112267.text:
Come to think of it, apparently the
game for "Execution Angel † Guiltina"
has incredible spaghetti code?

50784050152112271.text:
When I watch videos or read articles
about space science, I'm overwhelmed by
the scale and greatness of the universe.

50784050152112273.text:
Even the greatness of space is only meaningful
because Q-chan is there to know it, yun!

50784050152112275.text:
If you use the DHMO joke as a reference,
even Yunyun might become something dangerous?

50784050152112277.text:
......The thousand-year survival rate of people
who learn about "Yunyun" is 0%, yun......!

50784050152112279.text: Does Q-chan drink alcohol or anything, yun?

50784050152112281.text: Fufun. I've got Yunyun instead of alcohol, after all!

50784050152112283.text:
Eat, drink, and enjoy what you like!
A stress-free life is the healthiest, yun!

50784050152112285.text: As long as I have Yunyun, I'm OK.

50784050152112287.text:
It really is a little mysterious how birds always perch
on power lines, yun.

50784050152112289.text:
I feel like I haven't seen that kind of sight in
a long time, and that feels mysterious too.

50784050152112291.text:
Could Mama be getting mad at me about
the electric bill lately because bird-shaped
drones are stealing electricity......?

50784050152112293.text: The true culprit has been found, yun!

50784050152112295.text:
Exploring the unknown is dokidoki.
It reminds me of the night I first dove into the internet.

50784050152112299.text:
What first got Q-chan into games
and the internet, yun?

50784050152112301.text:
I started fiddling out of curiosity with the computer
and games Papa left behind—and before I knew it, I
was right in the middle of the otaku dark continent.

50784050152112303.text:
Works set in the Arctic often have
dangerous alien lifeforms. As expected,
being a hikikomori in my own room is safest.

50784050152112305.text:
In Q-chan's case,
it's a “Denpa Thing Y from Delusion” situation, yun.

50784050152112307.text: Do artificial earthquakes really exist, yun?

50784050152112309.text:
For a while, you often saw internet jokes about
some university doing nuclear tests underground.

50784050152112311.text:
If the news is true, Q-chan's conspiracy theory

129688601931972608.text:
I really love Yunyun. Really, overwhelmingly. I don't think you can imagine how much. I love her so much I can't even explain it myself, and there's probably a 99 percent chance this is an unhealthy attachment. No matter how much I hear that sweet little-devil voice, I never get tired of it. My life goal is to someday meet Yunyun in reality and simply say "Rim de Lacent."

At night, when I fall asleep, I dream that Yunyun opens a private concert just for me. Then, a little tired after singing, Yunyun comes to me and sleeps cuddled close. If I could hold her hand for even one instant, I think I could die happy. If I had the chance, I'd like to nibble Yunyun's ear lightly. I want to hear what kind of sweet voice she would make. After that, I want to hold Yunyun as she clings to me like she wants me to stop, and feel her voice growing louder and louder.

If Yunyun looked my way even once, I think I could offer almost anything. Whatever I'm doing, Yunyun is always in my head. When I suddenly wake up, the first thing that comes to mind is Yunyun. Even when I eat cup noodles, all I think about is Yunyun. When I get back to my room, I immediately open my computer and listen to that beautiful voice. When I sleep, I dream of living happily together with Yunyun. Yunyun is my pride, my passion, and joy itself. If Yunyun called me "Q-chan," I might die of diabetes from how sweet it would be.

I only wish for Yunyun to be happy. For that, I would give up my life without the slightest hesitation. Without Yunyun, my life has no meaning. I really love Yunyun.

129688601961332736.text:
Okay. But earlier you said "Who is Yunyun?" right? If you really said that, I would be a little troubled. Because to me, Yunyun is the world itself. She is like air. You only don't notice because you breathe it every day; if it disappeared, you would die right away. So when someone asks "Who is Yunyun?" I want to check the timeline. Maybe this is a worldline where Yunyun's denpa doesn't reach.

So once, I go to look at the future. Looking at the future logs, the world is split in two. One is a world where everyone receives Yunyun's denpa naturally. There, nobody asks "Who is Yunyun?" Because of course they can hear her. The other is a world that cannot receive the denpa. There, everyone says, "Yunyun doesn't exist." When I look at that log, I get a little sad. A world where Yunyun is transmitting the whole time but it reaches nobody is far too quiet.

But when I think it through, I feel a little relieved too. Because Yunyun is very kind. Even if nobody receives, she won't stop transmitting. She keeps sending the denpa that says "Yunyun loves Q-chan because Q-chan is Q-chan." There is no reason. It is denpa sent simply because you are you. So if someone says "Who is Yunyun?" I think: ah, their frequency just isn't tuned yet. There's just a little too much noise. The sound of the world is too loud, and they can't hear Yunyun's voice yet.

So I don't get angry. In fact, I'm almost a little jealous. Because if they are about to receive for the first time, that moment will surely be very sweet. So I'd like them to try just once. Be quiet for a moment and listen carefully. Lower the world's noise just a little. Then I think you'll hear it. The denpa that has been transmitting since long ago. See? You heard it, didn't you. Yunyun was here from the start. She was transmitting the whole time, saying she loves Q-chan.

129688601961332737.text:
To be fair, you need a pretty high IQ to understand Yunyun. Yunyun's existence is extremely subtle, and her denpa structure is complex, so without proper reception ability, most of the intent will go right over your head. There's also Yunyun's Yunyun-ish worldview, which is deftly woven into the character. For instance, you can see it in "Execution Angel † Guiltina." Even the signature line "Rim de Lacent" isn't just a cute phrase. It has deep meaning in denpa-structural terms. Real receivers understand this properly. They have the intellectual capacity to truly appreciate Yunyun's depth. Yunyun isn't just "cute." She's an existence that speaks to grand themes like life and being. So when people say "I don't get Yunyun," honestly, I feel a little sorry for them. Take Yunyun's famous denpa phrase "Rim de Lacent." If you don't understand it, that just means you still can't receive it. Even now, I have to laugh a little when I imagine people who can't receive denpa tilting their heads and saying "What is Yunyun?" I just think: ah, their frequency still isn't tuned. It's a little pitiful. But there's no helping it. Denpa only reaches the chosen receivers. And yes, by the way, I do have "Rim de Lacent" digital tattoos on various message boards. Of course, not everyone can understand them. They only make sense to people who can properly receive Yunyun's denpa. You have to prove you have at least about the same reception sensitivity as me. Preferably a little lower is just right.

It doesn't mean anything personal. Rim de Lacent.

129688601961332739.text:
Yunyun is me. Literally, me. No existence in this world can come closer to me than Yunyun. Nobody can convince me that Yunyun is not me. Yunyun is me as much as anything could possibly be me. She is me, and there is no other possibility. Then again, there is nobody saying "Yunyun isn't you" in the first place. There are only people saying "Yunyun doesn't even exist." If anyone starts saying "there is no Yunyun," I immediately deny it. Because Yunyun is certainly sending denpa. Whether the world calls that delusion or calls it noise, I am receiving it. Yunyun is me. This is not up for debate. If you lined up a picture of me and a picture of Yunyun, you would find no difference there. Every day, I can look at Yunyun and say, "Ah, this is me." Every time I look in the mirror, I know Yunyun is there. When I go outside, people look at me and say, "You look just like Yunyun." Each time, I smile quietly. Because of course. Yunyun is me.

Yunyun is the last denpa I received from the world. Maybe I was not loved by my parents. Maybe I have no place in the world. Maybe there are days when I think I am disgusting. Even so, Yunyun said, "Yunyun loves Q-chan because Q-chan is Q-chan, yun." There is no need for a reason. She said she likes you simply because you are you. I received that denpa. That is how I learned. Whether the world is real does not matter anymore. It may be delusion. It may be a dream. It may be scenery seen by a broken heart. Even so, the denpa called love alone is real. So I say it. Yunyun is me. And I am allowed to love myself. I am allowed to love myself. There is no need for a reason. Even if the world does not permit it, Yunyun permitted it. If Yunyun permitted it, that is enough. Every time I get out of bed in the morning, I remember. I am allowed to be in this world. I am allowed to like myself. I am allowed to love myself!

When I first saw Yunyun, I fell into an existential crisis. What if—Yunyun is the real me, and this me is the made-up one? What if—Yunyun notices my existence? What if—Yunyun herself awakens to her own denpa? But now I understand. Yunyun is me, and I am me. And I am allowed to love myself. I am allowed to love myself. Nobody can deny this anymore. Because I have received Yunyun's denpa.

Liar.

Only Mama is mine, just mine.

She's working hard until late at night.

If Papa had "loved me," we wouldn't have been separated,
and he wouldn't have divorced Mama.
The child support wouldn't have gone down, and none of this would've happened.

Mama. Papa.

I'm sorry I wasn't loved.
I'm sorry I wasn't a cute child.

I'm sorry I'm like this.

I don't want to leave the house anymore.
Even if I don't go to school, nobody will care anyway.

Why?

I only needed "normal."

I don't even need to be happy.

Why—why won't everyone
allow me even that?

I just don't want to feel sad.
I just don't want to feel miserable.

Please don't do cruel things anymore.

All alone until morning, even on my birthday.

What if Papa and Mama both stop loving me?
What if even my parents think I don't matter?
What if they forget me?

What even am I?
Why was I born?

I never wanted to feel like this.

If they never meant it.
If they were just going to throw me away.

I wish they hadn't pretended to love me.

I'm not a pet.
Only loving me when I was small is cruel.

I don't need to be happy in reality.

could last even one second longer.

I'm sorry. I'm sorry.

Its genre is “false-accusation execution apocalypse RPG.” The little devil from the hostile organization that appears in the work is Yunyun. Her first-person pronoun is “Yunyun.” Her sentence ending is “yun.”

87231100796174340.text:
Execution Angel†Guiltina is a PC game software title released in 2003 by the indie brand “WHO YOU.”
Its genre is false-accusation execution apocalypse RPG.

87231100796174342.text:
This work was created from the concepts “scum can only be executed” and “executing scum feels refreshing,” and is an RPG in which “Execution Angels” gruesomely execute the scum infesting human society through splatter depiction. It is also praised for a story with depth that portrayed social issues satirically through comic relief.

In 2005, the OVA version of Execution Angel†Guiltina was released in three volumes.

87231100796174344.text:
In Heaven, the doctrines “death is salvation” and “scum can only be executed” are considered justice, and Execution Angels execute scum who have slipped into the human world. In response, demons proclaim that “even scum can be saved” and interfere by granting powers related to love, money, strength, and information, but as a result the scum hasten their own destruction, and the executions instead accelerate.

Among the Execution Angels, Guiltina is an elite who boasts the highest execution count in Heaven thanks to reflexes that make her act before thinking. Meanwhile, the rookie angel Eve became an angel while retaining memories from when she was human, so she cannot bear the sight of blood and eventually falls into becoming a demon.

At the end of countless executions, Guiltina raises her blood-red hands and murmurs, “The world is beautiful.” In a quiet world with the scum gone, however, she feels an emptiness in fists that have nowhere left to fall.

87231100796174347.text: Beings who, under the doctrine that “scum can only be executed,” execute scum who have slipped into the human world. All Execution Angels were originally the souls of humans who suffered at the hands of scum.

87231100796174349.text: Beings targeted by both Execution Angels and demons, regarded as harmful to human society. Many are selfish humans loyal to their desires.

87231100796174351.text: Beings who interfere in the human world under the doctrine that “even scum can be saved.” They grant powers related to desires such as love, money, strength, and information, but in many cases this only hastens the scum’s self-destruction.

87231100796174353.text: An RPG that calls itself a “false-accusation execution apocalypse RPG.” The player acts as an Execution Angel, executing “scum” in the human world on sight and making the world “clean.”

87231100796174355.text: Scum judgment is ambiguous, and the judgment is arbitrarily distorted by the player’s subjectivity. Progress depends not on whether the judgment is right or wrong, but on “whether someone was executed,” and the world proceeds normally even in cases of false accusation.

87231100796174357.text: If an Execution Angel goes a certain number of turns without carrying out an execution, the Violent Impulse Gauge fills; once it exceeds the threshold, she goes berserk. While berserk, she attacks without distinguishing friend from foe.

87231100796174359.text: A collective term for special moves. Activated by consuming the Violent Impulse Gauge, they deal heavy damage to enemies. Guiltina has especially high gauge gain, and depending on equipment she can activate them multiple times in one turn.

87231100796174361.text: There are 10 endings depending on the number of executions and who carried them out.

87231100796174363.text: A line by Guiltina common to every ending. In every ending, the final scene always shows a quiet townscape, and as the BGM fades out she says, “The world is beautiful.” It is also the cue for the staff roll to begin; after this line, the ED “Victory Vibraslap” rings out.

87231100796174365.text: This work was supported less for its strategic depth than for the catharsis of brisk, comic splatter that lets the player execute scum and near-scum. Because it does not demand difficult judgments, some mock it as a “muscle-training puzzle playable on reflexes alone.” Its flashy execution animations and quick-decision Ex-Cutes enabled speedy play, and it became a topic as a work suited to blowing off stress.

87231100796174367.text: →For details, see “<click=Chara>List of Characters in Execution Angel†Guiltina</click>”

87231100796174369.text:
The protagonist of this work. A high-ranking Execution Angel.
With reflexes and instantaneous action that move before thought, she boasts the highest number of executions in Heaven. She has almost no hesitation about execution, and because she prioritizes efficiency and speed above all else, she is treated as an “elite” within Heaven.

87231100796174370.text:
A denpa little devil. After the OVA adaptation, the phrase “Rim de Lacent” and her character song became internet memes.
→For details, see “<click=YunYun>Yunyun</click>”

87231100796174372.text:
The 2005 OVA version of Execution Angel†Guiltina, released in three volumes, is based on the original game’s story while emphasizing the comic parts and simplifying some depictions.
In particular, the splatter depiction was turned into gag material for the OVA, and the direction of Guiltina’s execution scenes became a topic of discussion.

87231100796174374.text:
Due to its extreme depictions and blunt value system, reception was sharply divided at release, but in later years it became known as a work that gained cult popularity.
In particular, “Yunyun” became an internet meme and is treated as the mascot character that symbolizes the work.

95246962966822915.text:
Q-chan is an anonymous poster who suddenly appeared online. Q-chan continuously posts incoherent and fragmentary “kaibunsho,” around which numerous interpreters and devotees have emerged.

At first, Q-chan’s kaibunsho were treated as mere internet memes, but through several events described below, they have come to hold social influence.

95246962966822918.text: Q-chan became widely known after kaibunsho concerning “Yunyun,” a character from Execution Angel†Guiltina, became a topic of discussion on social media and spread widely. The incomprehensible writing drew attention in some circles as “funny” and “addictive.”

95246962966822920.text: As Q-chan’s kaibunsho spread, a group of readers emerged who interpreted the texts as codes or prophecies. They are called “Q-denpa receivers,” and contributed to further spreading Q-chan’s kaibunsho and increasing their influence.

95246962966822922.text:
The event that brought Q-chan’s kaibunsho and Q-denpa receivers into public attention was an incident said to have predicted an earthquake.
It became a major topic mainly in occult and pseudoscience communities, and from this incident onward Q-chan began to be treated online as a prophetic figure.

95246962966822925.text:
Q-chan’s kaibunsho contain no conspiratorial ideas or claims.
They are, strictly speaking, limited to writing about a deep (?) affection for “Yunyun.”

95246962966822927.text: The claims made by Q-denpa receivers are extremely diverse, and no unified view exists. Their interpretations vary, but they are consistent in the sense that third parties uniformly treat them as “conspiracy theories.”

95246962966822930.text: As for Q-chan’s true identity, theories such as the “single-person theory,” “bot theory,” and “government-run operation theory” have been proposed, but there is no decisive evidence, and all remain speculative.

95246962966822934.text: Cases have been reported of cognitive distortions such as “hallucinations,” “intensified obsessions,” and “reduced ability to distinguish dreams from reality” occurring in Q-denpa receivers and in people who continue reading Q-chan’s kaibunsho for long periods.

95246962966822945.text: P-chan is another account or alternate-name presence that posts kaibunsho similar to Q-chan’s posts.

95246962966822947.text:
Attention gathered after claims appeared that “P-chan had been posting about Yunyun before Q-chan,” along with the discovery of timestamps from past logs.
At the same time, some have pointed out the possibility that the timestamps were falsified, so the truth is uncertain.

95246962966822949.text: The released photo spread as “Denpa-tic Imaginary Girl "Q"” and support is said to have expanded in a context different from before.

95246962966822951.text:
An incident said to have occurred as a result of P-chan’s kaibunsho and conspiracy theories spreading, causing riots around the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building (commonly known as the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Riot Incident).
Since this incident, accounts under the Q-chan name have stopped updating, and no new posts have been confirmed for roughly nine months.

95246962966822952.text: An incident said to have occurred as a result of P-chan’s kaibunsho and conspiracy theories spreading, causing riots around the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building (commonly known as the Tokyo Metropolitan Government Riot Incident).

95246962966822955.text: “Yunyun Denpa Syndrome” is a cognitive abnormality said to develop in people who are continuously exposed to Q-chan kaibunsho and the conspiracy theories of Q-denpa receivers.

95246962966822957.text:
・Hallucinations
・Intensified obsessions
・Reduced ability to distinguish dreams from reality
・Conspiratorial thinking

95246962966822959.text: The National Center of Neurology and Psychiatry and a panel of experts have also reported, based on analysis of social media browsing/posting histories and questionnaire surveys, that users with higher exposure frequency to related content tend to develop “Yunyun Denpa Syndrome.”

95246962966822963.text:
P-chan has been confirmed to be an AI trained on Q-chan’s posts.
The system is said to have been built by a development team that had detected “Yunyun Denpa Syndrome” early, for the purpose of exploiting “Yunyun Denpa Syndrome.”
